title: Red Group FAIL: ORBIT_PERIOD_WRONG — claimed period=12 for m=9 (F^12=−I≢I)
_fixture_purpose: Canonical confusion fail. For m=9, F^12=[[8,0],[0,8]]=−I mod 9 ≢ I. A common error is to claim period=12 because F^12·x=−x, mistaking −I for I (confusing projective order with linear order). The validator checks F^12 mod 9 = −I ≢ I, so ORBIT_PERIOD_WRONG fires. Note: the PROJECTIVE order (in PGL₂(Z/9Z)) is indeed 12 — the LINEAR order in GL₂(Z/9Z) is 24.

red_group._error: WRONG: claimed period=12 (projective order), but linear order in GL₂(Z/9Z) is 24; F^12=[[8,0],[0,8]]=-I ≢ I

validation_checks.details: RG5 already failed; minimality vacuous here

validation_checks.description: orbit_type ∈ valid set AND period=1 ↔ singularity
validation_checks.details: orbit_type='cosmos', period=12≠1 — type consistent with wrong period
